  "findings": [
    "Milky/milkshake residue on oil filler cap indicates coolant mixing with engine oil — emulsion present",
    "Coolant intrusion into lubrication system — likely failed head gasket, cracked cylinder head, or failed oil cooler",
    "Continued operation risks catastrophic engine bearing failure from loss of lubricity"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop engine immediately — do not operate asset further",
    "Perform cooling system pressure test and inspect for combustion gases in coolant",
    "Diagnose head gasket, cylinder head, and oil cooler integrity before any reuse",
    "Drain and inspect oil and coolant for cross-contamination extent",
    "Replace failed sealing component and flush both systems before return to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "A milkshake oil cap is a definitive field cue for coolant-oil emulsion, meaning a critical internal leak path exists (head gasket, cylinder head crack, or oil cooler breach). This is an installed-defective condition at rough-complete stage — the asset is assembled and operating but has a serious fluid cross-contamination defect. Continued operation will destroy the engine. OEM specs govern coolant and oil system separation; any emulsion on the oil cap violates that requirement.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service specs — engine oil and coolant system separation requirements",
    "OEM cooling system pressure test procedure",
    "OEM head gasket and oil cooler inspection criteria"
  ]
}
